published.text:
### Hero
Heading: Build vs. Buy AI Automation
Body: Every business owner considering automation faces the same question: do I buy an off-the-shelf tool or hire someone to build a custom system? The answer depends on your workflow complexity, budget, and how unique your processes are. This page gives you an honest framework to decide — without pushing you toward either option.
Sub: Below: cost comparison, customization tradeoffs, time-to-value, and when each path makes sense.

### Core sections
- Three paths to AI automation (cards: Build custom, Buy off-the-shelf, Hybrid)
- Side-by-side comparison (table: upfront cost, ongoing cost, customization, time to value, ownership, integration depth)
- When to build vs when to buy (when-to-use)
- What most businesses get wrong (considerations: over-building, under-buying, vendor lock-in, maintenance)
- Case studies referencing all 3 existing projects

### FAQ
Subhead: Practical answers about building vs. buying AI automation

### Final CTA
Heading: Not sure whether to build or buy?
Body: Book a 30-minute call. We will look at your specific workflows and give you an honest recommendation on the right path.

edits.description: Published as part of batch 4. Core decision-stage page for buyers evaluating automation approach.
Interlinks to consultant page, consultant vs agency, ROI guide, pricing, no-tech-team page.
Frames hybrid (SaaS + custom glue) as the most common winning pattern.
Honest about when off-the-shelf SaaS is the right answer.
